title: Geopolitical risk is at its highest level in modern history
subtitle: Daily Geopolitical Risk Index, 1985-2019 = 100
source: Dario Caldara and Matteo Iacoviello, Federal Reserve Board, Goldman Sachs GIR
editorialPoint: The 2026 Iran War has pushed geopolitical risk beyond 9/11, beyond the Iraq invasion, beyond Russia's invasion of Ukraine. We are in uncharted territory.
methodology: Index counts the number of articles related to geopolitical tensions in 11 US, UK, and Canadian newspapers. Monthly index from 1900 uses NYT, Wall Street Journal, and Financial Times archives.

keyEvents.label: US airstrike kills Iran's top general
US/Israel strikes on Iran — Hormuz crisis

note: The current reading exceeds 9/11 levels. The index from 1900 shows only WWI, WWII, and the Cuban Missile Crisis reaching comparable levels.
